content.text:
```mdl
@ Contract: Non‑Disclosure Agreement (NDA) – “BROOKS’ BOTTLING COMPANY, LLC v. CLIENT”
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_12_nda_16

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Types
# ------------------------------------------------------------
type Party = {
    name: string,               # e.g. "BROOKS" or "CLIENT"
    is_receiving_party: bool   # true for the party that receives Confidential Information
}

type ConfidentialMaterial = {
    description: string,   # free‑form description of the material
    is_returned: bool,      # true if the material has been handed back to CLIENT
    is_destroyed: bool,     # true if the material has been destroyed / disposed
    retained_years: int,    # number of years the material has been kept after termination
    notice_given: bool      # true if CLIENT has received the required 10‑day written notice
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities
# ------------------------------------------------------------
@ Section 1 – Parties
entity brooks: Party = { name = "BROOKS", is_receiving_party = true }
entity client: Party = { name = "CLIENT", is_receiving_party = false }

@ Section 5 – Confidential material that BROOKS holds
entity material: ConfidentialMaterial = {
    description = "Confidential records, data, information and other documents",
    is_returned = false,
    is_destroyed = false,
    retained_years = 0,
    notice_given = false
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper functions
# ------------------------------------------------------------
func client_requested_return() -> bool:
    # The NDA does not give a concrete flag; we model the request as a boolean
    # that can be set by the environment (e.g. a test harness).  The function
    # exists only to keep the rule readable.
    false   # default: no written request (can be overridden in a concrete trace)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Rules – obligations of the Receiving Party (BROOKS)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
@ Section 5 – Return of material upon termination
rule O return_or_destroy_on_termination:
    # The termination event is implicit – the rule is evaluated at the step
    # where the agreement has ended.  The obligation is:
    #   * If CLIENT requests the material, BROOKS must return it immediately.
    #   * If CLIENT does not request it, BROOKS may retain it for up to 3 years,
    #     after which it must give CLIENT a 10‑day written notice and then
    #     destroy the material.
    let termination: bool = true               # the rule is scoped to the termination step
    let request: bool = client_requested_return()
    let must_return: bool = request implies material.is_returned
    let must_destroy: bool =
        (not request) implies
            (material.retained_years >= 3 and material.notice_given and material.is_destroyed)
    (termination implies (must_return and must_destroy)) always

@ Section 2 – Prohibition on disclosure (for completeness)
rule F disclose_confidential_information:
    # BROOKS, its directors, employees, agents and representatives shall not
    # disclose Confidential Information except as permitted in Sections 3 or 4.
    true always   # placeholder – the actual disclosure logic would be modelled elsewhere

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Optional: a fact to instantiate a concrete scenario (used only for testing)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# fact material.retained_years = 4
# fact material.notice_given = true
# fact material.is_destroyed = true
# fact client_requested_return = false
```
